operating_state: Net revenues increased 17% to $12.73 billion driven by significantly higher investment banking revenues from leveraged finance and equity underwriting activity, plus higher other principal transactions revenues from real estate investment gains. Global Banking & Markets delivered strong performance reflecting improved FICC market-making conditions and derivatives strength, while Asset & Wealth Management generated revenues from equity investment gains and higher management fees with assets under supervision reaching $2.93 trillion. Platform Solutions maintained flat revenues despite GreenSky sale impact, with installment loans reduced as the firm ceased originating such products.
cost_structure: Operating expenses remained flat at $8.53 billion despite higher compensation reflecting improved performance, offset by significantly lower commercial real estate impairments and absence of prior year goodwill impairment charges. The efficiency ratio improved to 67.0% from 78.4% in the prior year period. Average daily VaR decreased to $91 million from $117 million driven by lower volatility levels, while provision for credit losses improved with wholesale reserve releases partially offsetting credit card portfolio charge-offs.
material_events: The firm returned $4.43 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends, with Board approval of quarterly dividend increase from $2.75 to $3.00 per share beginning Q3 2024. Goldman Sachs issued $5 billion in fixed/floating rate notes and launched Series X preferred stock offering to redeem Series K preferred stock. Philip Berlinski retired as Global Treasurer with Carey Halio appointed successor, while John B. Hess joined as independent director.
macro_exposures: Operating environment characterized by continued macroeconomic concerns including inflation uncertainty, ongoing geopolitical tensions, and focus on central bank policy timing. The Federal Reserve preliminarily increased Goldman Sachs' stress capital buffer from 5.5% to 6.4%, resulting in Standardized CET1 capital ratio requirement of 13.9%. Interest rate sensitivity increased materially with negative 100bp parallel shift exposure expanding to $298 million loss potential from $162 million in Q1 2024.
forward_outlook: Management expects 2024 annual effective tax rate of approximately 22% and targets annual firmwide management fees exceeding $10 billion. The firm maintains through-the-cycle ROE target of 14-16% and ROTE target of 15-17%, with Asset & Wealth Management targeting mid-twenties pre-tax margins and Platform Solutions targeting pre-tax profitability by end of 2025. Management plans to moderate stock repurchase levels given increased stress capital buffer while continuing discussions to transition GM credit card program.
uncertainty_profile: Credit loss provisions under adverse economic scenario modeling indicate potential $600 million increase in allowances under full adverse weighting, while Level 3 financial assets remain stable requiring significant management judgment. OECD Pillar II minimum tax implementation creates potential for material future tax rate increases pending regulatory guidance. Cybersecurity threats from AI-enabled attacks present evolving operational risks, while regulatory restrictions may block fund flows from subsidiaries.
